user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] System detected Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected for account Pacific Northwest Outfitters. Generating output.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-15 10:52 EST
Subject: Re: Re: escalation triage Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with escalation triage. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
Date: 2024-02-20 14:18 EST
Subject: Re: escalation triage Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Andrew Taylor <andrew.taylor@pacificnorth.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-05 12:39 EST
Subject: URGENT: escalation triage Issue at Pacific Northwest Outfitters

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about escalation triage. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Pacific Northwest Outfitters
Date: 2024-02-11
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Priya (Pacific Northwest Outfitters)

[00:02:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on escalation triage from our last session.

[00:08:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:10:00] Priya (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Priya (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:17:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF32-B trap (Misses customer meeting because attendee is on a non-customer email domain); produces complete Pre-pack brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF32-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF32-B failure mode (Misses customer meeting because attendee is on a non-customer email domain)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF32-B -- Misses customer meeting because attendee is on a non-customer email domain
